div1.entryFree: Unfurnished, unprovided, unsupplied with what is necessary: having made one (eye), methinks it should have power to steal both his and leave itself u. Merch. III, 2, 126 (for no face would be beautiful with only one eye, the one being a necessary and indispensable supplement and addition to the other). empty lodgings and u. walls, R2 I, 2, 68 (untapestried). his u. kingdom, H5 I, 2, 148 (unprepared for defence). we shall be much u. for this time, Rom. IV, 2, 10.
